description: Add NPC dialogue system with voice lines for Barkeep and Patron in the tavern. This includes: extending DialogueLine with optional audioPath, adding NPC_DIALOGUE SlicePhase, implementing NPC interaction in tavernScene, adding NPC-specific BarkEvents for the Barkeep (pre-sewer and post-sewer) and Patron, and updating RETURN_LINES with Barkeep post-sewer audio.
steps: 1. Extend DialogueLine data class in composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/ui/rpg/DialogueLine.kt: add `val audioPath: String? = null` as a third field. This keeps backward compat since existing DialogueLine() calls without audioPath still work.

2. Update DialogueOverlay composable in the same file to accept a `barkAudioPlayer: BarkAudioPlayer?` parameter (defaulting to null). When a dialogue line displays and has a non-null audioPath, call the audio player's underlying AudioPlayer.play(audioPath). Import BarkAudioPlayer and use its internal audioPlayer via a new `playRawPath(path: String)` method to be added to BarkAudioPlayer.

3. Add a `playRawPath(path: String)` method to BarkAudioPlayer in composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/rpg/bark/audio/BarkAudioPlayer.kt. This allows playing an arbitrary WAV path (for NPC lines that are not BarkEvent-based). It should stop any current playback and play the given path.
4. Add NPC_DIALOGUE to SlicePhase enum in composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/rpg/SlicePhase.kt (insert after TAVERN). This phase represents when an NPC dialogue overlay is active in the tavern.

5. Add new BarkEvent entries to composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/rpg/bark/BarkEvent.kt in a new NPC Dialogue section: BARKEEP_SPEND_SOME_COIN, BARKEEP_BEEN_PLAYING_IN_SEWERS, PATRON_HE_SURE_IS_SLOW. These represent NPC voice lines triggered during dialogue.

6. Add BarkRegistry definitions for the 3 new BarkEvent entries in composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/rpg/bark/BarkRegistry.kt. Use PartyCharacter.BRUGG for barkeep lines (gruff = Brugg voice) and PartyCharacter.VELLUM for patron (raspy). Mark them as SAFE_BARK, CURRENT_ROOM scope, 0s cooldown (dialogue-driven, not player-fired), usedInSlice=true.

7. Add BarkAudioRegistry entries for the 3 new events in composeApp/src/commonMain/kotlin/rpg/bark/BarkAudioRegistry.kt: BARKEEP_SPEND_SOME_COIN -> 'spend_some_coin_or_get_out.wav', BARKEEP_BEEN_PLAYING_IN_SEWERS -> 'been_playing_in_the_sewers_have_we.wav', PATRON_HE_SURE_IS_SLOW -> 'he_sure_is_slow_for_a_four_armed_bartender.wav'.

8. In SliceScreen.kt, implement tavernScene.onEntityInteraction: When entity.id == 'barkeep', check if the game has returned from the sewer (use a tracked boolean `hasReturnedFromSewer` set true when phase transitions from RETURN_CUTSCENE to VICTORY or when RETURN_LINES finish). Pre-sewer: set dialogueLines to BARKEEP_PRE_SEWER_LINES, dialogueIndex=0, phase=NPC_DIALOGUE. Post-sewer: set dialogueLines to BARKEEP_POST_SEWER_LINES. When entity.id == 'patron': set dialogueLines to PATRON_LINES, phase=NPC_DIALOGUE.

9. Define the NPC dialogue line lists in SliceScreen.kt: BARKEEP_PRE_SEWER_LINES = [DialogueLine('Barkeep', 'Spend some coin or get out.', 'bark/brugg/spend_some_coin_or_get_out.wav'), DialogueLine('Brugg', 'Barkeep! A flagon of ale!', 'bark/brugg/barkeep_a_flagon_of_ale.wav')]. BARKEEP_POST_SEWER_LINES = [DialogueLine('Barkeep', 'Been playing in the sewers, have we?', 'bark/brugg/been_playing_in_the_sewers_have_we.wav')]. PATRON_LINES = [DialogueLine('Patron', 'He sure is slow for a four-armed bartender.', 'bark/vellum/he_sure_is_slow_for_a_four_armed_bartender.wav')].

10. Handle SlicePhase.NPC_DIALOGUE in the when(phase) block of SliceContent: render the ExploreView for the tavern WITH the DialogueOverlay on top, passing barkAudioPlayer to it. When dialogue finishes (advanceDialogue hits end of NPC lines), revert phase back to TAVERN.

11. Update RETURN_LINES in SliceScreen.kt: change the Barkeep line to include audioPath: DialogueLine('Barkeep', 'Back already? Smells like sewer.', 'bark/brugg/been_playing_in_the_sewers_have_we.wav'). This maps the scripted text to the closest matching WAV.

12. Wire the DialogueOverlay to receive and use the barkAudioPlayer. In SliceScreen.kt, pass the barkAudioPlayer to DialogueOverlay. In DialogueOverlay, when a new line is displayed (index changes), if the line has audioPath, call barkAudioPlayer.playRawPath(audioPath).

13. Add tests in composeApp/src/commonTest/kotlin/NpcDialogueTest.kt: Test that DialogueLine with audioPath retains the path. Test that NPC BarkEvent entries exist in BarkRegistry and BarkAudioRegistry. Test that BarkAudioPlayer.playRawPath routes to the underlying AudioPlayer.
acceptance_criteria: DialogueLine data class has an optional audioPath field defaulting to null
BarkAudioPlayer has a playRawPath(path: String) method that delegates to AudioPlayer.play()
BarkEvent.BARKEEP_SPEND_SOME_COIN, BARKEEP_BEEN_PLAYING_IN_SEWERS, and PATRON_HE_SURE_IS_SLOW are registered in BarkRegistry and BarkAudioRegistry
tavernScene.onEntityInteraction triggers NPC dialogue based on entity.id and game state
DialogueOverlay plays audio when a line has audioPath
RETURN_LINES includes the Barkeep's post-sewer line with audioPath
NPC_DIALOGUE phase shows dialogue overlay on top of tavern view and returns to TAVERN when done
All existing and new tests pass with ./gradlew desktopTest
verification: Run ./gradlew desktopTest and confirm all tests pass
Verify new NpcDialogueTest.kt tests cover DialogueLine.audioPath, BarkAudioPlayer.playRawPath, and NPC bark registry entries
findings: Added 3 new BarkEvent entries which required updating: (1) QuestbookProcessor exhaustive when-expression (added to flavor-only group), (2) BarkRegistryTest count assertions (59->62, 7->10 slice barks), (3) BarkAudioRegistryTest count assertion (59->62). The full ./gradlew build fails due to missing Android SDK (ANDROID_HOME) in the sandbox environment, but ./gradlew desktopTest compiles all common code and passes all 101+ tests successfully.
